sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To create and configure a stream processing workspace, you must have the
following:

- An Atlas [Manage Project Access](manage-projects)

- An Atlas user with the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>),
  the [Project Stream Processing Owner](<Project Stream Processing Owner>),
  or the [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) role to manage a Stream Processing Workspace.

  > **Note:** While the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>), [Project Stream Processing Owner](<Project Stream Processing Owner>),
  > and [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) roles all grant the privileges to create, view, edit and delete
  > stream processing workspaces, the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) role also allows users to perform tasks
  > such as creating database deployments, managing project access, and more.
  >
  > See [project role](project-roles) to learn more about the
  > differences between these roles.

sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
You must set a **default tier** when you create a stream processing workspace.
Stream processors that you create in this workspace
default to this tier. You can optionally set a
**max tier size** as well. You can't create stream
processors larger than the max tier in this workspace.

Defaults:

- **default tier**: `SP10`

- **max tier size**: unset

You can configure the default and max stream processing workspace tiers
only from the Atlas control plane (UI, API, Terraform, or CLI).
You can't configure the default and tiers from the data plane
([mongosh](mongodb-shell)).

sections.heading: View stream processing workspaces
sections.body:
To view your stream processing workspaces:

**View-spi-cli:**

Atlas CLI

To list all stream processing workspaces in the project you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas api streams listStreamWorkspaces [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas api streams listStreamWorkspaces](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-streams-listStreamWorkspaces).

**View-spi-ui:**

Atlas UI

**View-spi-api:**

Atlas Administration API

The Atlas Administration API provides different endpoints for
retrieving one or all stream processing workspaces.

[Return One Stream Workspace](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroupstreamworkspace)

[List All Stream Workspaces](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupstreamworkspaces)

sections.heading: Create a stream processing workspace
sections.body:
To create a new stream processing workspace:

**Create-spi-cli:**

Atlas CLI

To create one stream processing workspace in the project you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas api streams createStreamWorkspace [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas api streams createStreamWorkspace](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-streams-createStreamWorkspace).

**Create-spi-ui:**

Atlas UI

- Click Create workspace in the upper
  right corner.

- Configure your new stream processing workspace.

  - Select a default tier.

  - Select a Cloud Provider.

  - Select a Region from the dropdown menu.

  - Enter a string for the Workspace Name. Each
    stream processing workspace name must be unique within a project.

  - (Optional) Set a max tier size.

  To learn more about default tier and max tier sizes, see [Considerations](atlas-sp-default-tier-spw).

- Click Create.

**Create-spi-api:**

Atlas Administration API

The Atlas Administration API provides an endpoint for creating a
stream processing workspace.

[Create One Stream Workspace](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupstreamworkspace)

sections.heading: Delete a stream processing workspace
sections.body:
To delete a stream processing workspace:

**Delete-spi-cli:**

Atlas CLI

To delete one stream processing workspace in the project you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas api streams deleteStreamWorkspace [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas api streams deleteStreamWorkspace](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-streams-deleteStreamWorkspace).

**Delete-spi-ui:**

Atlas UI

- Locate the overview panel of the stream processing workspace you want to delete

- Click the ellipsis ... icon in the upper-right.

- Click Delete workspace.

- When prompted, type the name of the stream processing workspace in the text field.

- Click Delete.

**Delete-spi-api:**

Atlas Administration API

The Atlas Administration API provides an endpoint for deleting a
stream processing workspace.

[Delete One Stream Workspace](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deletegroupstreamworkspace)

sections.heading: Edit a stream processing workspace
sections.body:
To edit an stream processing workspace:

**Edit-spi-cli:**

Atlas CLI

To update one stream processing workspace in the project you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas api streams updateStreamWorkspace [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas api streams updateStreamWorkspace](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-streams-updateStreamWorkspace).

**Edit-spi-api:**

Atlas Administration API

The Atlas Administration API provides an endpoint for editing a
stream processing workspace.

[Update One Stream Workspace](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupstreamworkspace)

sections.heading: Download Audit Logs
sections.body:
To download your stream processing workspace audit logs:

**Download-log-spi-cli:**

Atlas CLI

To download audit logs for one stream processing workspace you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas api streams downloadAuditLogs [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas api streams downloadAuditLogs](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-api-streams-downloadAuditLogs).

**Download-log-spi-ui:**

Atlas UI

Click . From the dropdown
menu, select Audit Logs.

In the modal window, select a Time Period
from the dropdown menu. This value determines the logging
interval used to delimit events in the downloadable log file.

**Download-log-spi-api:**

Atlas Administration API

The Atlas Administration API provides an endpoint for creating a
stream processing workspace.

[Download Audit Logs for One Atlas Stream Processing Workspace](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-downloadgroupstreamauditlogs)
